keyPoints: Recall-first prompts before AI use grounded in retrieval-practice theory and TAP
Spacing and multiple retrieval attempts to maximize durability in AI-enabled learning
Immediate, informative feedback after recalls, ideally combining AI explanations with human oversight
Transfer-focused alignment between recall prompts and downstream AI tasks (near and far transfer goals)
Safeguards against AI overreliance, maintaining effortful recall and prompt verification
Delayed transfer assessments that reflect real-world problem solving
Rigorous randomized testing across domains to establish causal effects of recall-first AI prompts
